cards.back: When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure.
The totality of methods rationally arrived at and having absolute efficiency in every field of human activity.

cards.front: Who wrote 'The Humble Programmer' and what was his main argument?
cards.back: Edsger Dijkstra. He argued that programmers must approach their task with humility, respect the limitations of the human mind, and avoid clever tricks.
A programming methodology where the primary goal is to explain to humans what a computer should do, treating programs as works of literature.

cards.front: According to the Google Testing chapter, what is the recommended test pyramid ratio?
cards.back: 80% small/unit tests, 15% medium/integration tests, 5% large/end-to-end tests.
